question: Identify the Chicago library branch that ranked in the top five for circulation and visitors, and top three for computer sessions, every year from the year from 2017-2022 that has the highest total gasoline/gasohol sales across U.S. states to the year from 2017-2022 that has the highest total Charter flight passengers over Los Angeles international airports. What is the per capita income of the community area where this library is located? Please return the number only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering fuel_type = "Gasoline/gasohol" and summing value by year (from date) for 2017-2022, the maximum total is 294719877886 in 2019.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the highest total gasoline/gasohol sales? (Filter fuel_type == "Gasoline/gasohol"; derive year from date; sum value by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: Filtering FlightType = "Charter" and summing Passenger_Count by year (from ReportPeriod) for 2017-2022, the maximum total is 25993 in 2021.
2.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the highest total Charter flight passengers over Los Angeles international airports? (Filter FlightType == "Charter"; derive year from ReportPeriod; sum Passenger_Count by year and select the maximum.)

3.fact: In the 2019 Chicago Public Library circulation-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 5 by YTD circulation were: HAROLD WASHINGTON LIBRARY CENTER (581151), Sulzer Regional Library (369530), Edgewater (171399), Lincoln Belmont (168646), and Lincoln Park (133337).
3.subquestion: Which branches (with physical addresses) were in the top 5 by YTD circulation in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by LOCATION; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 5.)

4.fact: In the 2020 Chicago Public Library circulation-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 5 by YTD circulation were: Harold Washington Library Center (276878), Sulzer Regional Library (226464), Lincoln Belmont (105388), Edgewater (103570), and Northtown (94797).
4.subquestion: Which branches (with physical addresses) were in the top 5 by YTD circulation in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by BRANCH; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 5.)

5.subquestion: Which branches (with physical addresses) were in the top 5 by YTD circulation in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by BRANCH; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 5.)

6.fact: In the 2019 Chicago Public Library visitors-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 5 by YTD visitors were: Harold Washtington Library Center (1405964), Sulzer Regional Library (358644), Chinatown (271514), Woodson Regional Library (214173), and Edgewater (197045).
6.subquestion: Among the branches (from hop 1), which were in the top 5 by YTD visitors in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by LOCATION; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 5 overall.)

7.fact: In the 2020 Chicago Public Library visitors-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 5 by YTD visitors were: Harold Washtington Library Center (554374), Sulzer Regional Library (165519), Edgewater (91450), Woodson Regional Library (78366), and Rogers Park (75717).

8.fact: In the 2021 Chicago Public Library visitors-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 5 by YTD visitors were: Harold Washtington Library Center (543951), Sulzer Regional Library (175377), Edgewater (94307), Chinatown (93783), and Logan Square (92688).
8.subquestion: Among the branches (from hop 1), which were in the top 5 by YTD visitors in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by BRANCH; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 5 overall.)

9.fact: In the 2019 Chicago Public Library computer-sessions-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 3 by YTD computer sessions were: HAROLD WASHINGTON LIBRARY CENTER (209583), Woodson Regional Library (86153), and Sulzer Regional Library (75723).
9.subquestion: Among the branches (from hop 2), which were in the top 3 by YTD computer sessions in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by LOCATION; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 3 overall.)

10.fact: In the 2020 Chicago Public Library computer-sessions-by-location dataset (physical branches with non-null addresses), the top 3 by YTD computer sessions were: Harold Washington Library Center (78203), Woodson Regional Library (33365), and Sulzer Regional Library (27772).
10.subquestion: Among the branches (from hop 2), which were in the top 3 by YTD computer sessions in the given year? (Filter for this node: keep rows with non-null ADDRESS; group by BRANCH; sum YTD; sort YTD desc; take top 3 overall.)

12.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Conrad Sulzer Regional Library, the library is located in the Lincoln Square neighborhood at 4455 N. Lincoln Avenue.
12.subquestion: Which Chicago neighborhood is the library branch (from hop 3) located in? (Lookup: Conrad Sulzer Regional Library article.)

13.fact: In the Chicago life expectancy by community area dataset, Lincoln Square has Community Area Number 4.
13.subquestion: What community area number corresponds to the neighborhood from hop 4? (Filter for this node: Community Area == 'Lincoln Square'; return Community Area Number.)

14.subquestion: What is the per capita income for the community area from hop 5? (Filter for this node: Community Area == 4; return Per Capita Income.)
